perturbation.original_text: Vector hereby agrees that it and its Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the parties, that the Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and that Vector and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) Vector may make any disclosure of such information to which Captaris gives its prior written consent and (ii) any of such information may be disclosed by Vector to its Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating and implementing a possible transaction between the parties, who shall keep such information confidential and who are provided with a copy of this letter agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Vector hereby agrees that it and its Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the parties, that the Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and that Vector and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) Vector may make any disclosure of such information to which Captaris gives its prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting clause (ii), which allows disclosure to Representatives under specific conditions, the contract now implies that all disclosures require Captaris's consent, even to Vector's own representatives. This contradicts the practical need for Vector to share information internally to evaluate the transaction. The agreement now has in-text contradiction, which is found in the same paragraph, where it states that Vector can't disclose the information in any manner, but that's contradicted by the 'provided however'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vector hereby agrees that it and its Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the parties, that the Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and that Vector and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) Vector may make any disclosure of such information to which Captaris gives its prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed Vector to disclose information to its representatives under specific conditions (clause ii). The changed text omits this clause, implying that all disclosures, even to Vector's representatives, require Captaris's consent. This creates a contradiction because the original text permits certain disclosures without consent, while the changed text suggests no disclosures are permitted without consent. This directly impacts the contradicted text which states Vector can't disclose information, but that clause has exceptions. Removing exception (ii) directly contradicts this original intent and creates an in-text contradiction based on the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that (i) Vector or any of its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process or law or any rule of any self-regulatory organization) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material furnished by Captaris or (ii) Vector or any of its Representative or Captaris or any of its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process or law or any rule of any self-regulatory organization) to disclose any of the information described in the preceding paragraph (the “ Other Confidential Information ”)), then Vector or Captaris, as applicable (the “ Disclosure Party ”) shall, to the extent permitted by applicable law, provide Captaris or Vector, as applicable (the “ Other Party ”) with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement so that the Other Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this letter agreement. If, in the absence of any protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Other Party, the Disclosure Party or any of its Representatives determines in good faith, after consultation with the Disclosure Party’s outside counsel or such Representative’s counsel, as applicable, that it is legally compelled to disclose Evaluation Material (or any of the Other Confidential Information), the Disclosure Party or its Representative may, without liability hereunder, disclose only that portion of the Evaluation Material or Other Confidential Information that it determines in good faith, after consultation with such counsel, it is legally required to be disclosed, provided that the Disclosure Party exercise commercially reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the Other Confidential Information, including, without limitation, by cooperating with the Other Party to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that 2 confidential treatment will be accorded the Evaluation Material and the Other Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that (i) Vector or any of its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process or law or any rule of any self-regulatory organization) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material furnished by Captaris or (ii) Vector or any of its Representative or Captaris or any of its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process or law or any rule of any self-regulatory organization) to disclose any of the information described in the preceding paragraph (the “ Other Confidential Information ”)), then Vector or Captaris, as applicable (the “ Disclosure Party ”) shall, to the extent permitted by applicable law, provide Captaris or Vector, as applicable (the “ Other Party ”) with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement so that the Other Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this letter agreement. If, in the absence of any protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Other Party, the Disclosure Party or any of its Representatives determines in good faith, after consultation with the Disclosure Party’s outside counsel or such Representative’s counsel, as applicable, that it is legally compelled to disclose Evaluation Material (or any of the Other Confidential Information), the Disclosure Party or its Representative may, without liability hereunder, disclose only that portion of the Evaluation Material or Other Confidential Information that it determines in good faith, after consultation with such counsel, it is legally required to be disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'provided that...' clause regarding commercially reasonable efforts to preserve confidentiality, the section becomes contradictory. The Disclosure Party is still allowed to disclose if legally compelled, but the requirement to minimize the disclosure and seek confidential treatment is removed. This contradicts the overall confidentiality agreement, creating an in-text contradiction. The section now permits disclosure but eliminates the obligation to protect the information as much as possible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that (i) Vector or any of its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process or law or any rule of any self-regulatory organization) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material furnished by Captaris or (ii) Vector or any of its Representative or Captaris or any of its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process or law or any rule of any self-regulatory organization) to disclose any of the information described in the preceding paragraph (the “ Other Confidential Information ”)), then Vector or Captaris, as applicable (the “ Disclosure Party ”) shall, to the extent permitted by applicable law, provide Captaris or Vector, as applicable (the “ Other Party ”) with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement so that the Other Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this letter agreement. If, in the absence of any protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Other Party, the Disclosure Party or any of its Representatives determines in good faith, after consultation with the Disclosure Party’s outside counsel or such Representative’s counsel, as applicable, that it is legally compelled to disclose Evaluation Material (or any of the Other Confidential Information), the Disclosure Party or its Representative may, without liability hereunder, disclose only that portion of the Evaluation Material or Other Confidential Information that it determines in good faith, after consultation with such counsel, it is legally required to be disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a clause that mandates commercially reasonable efforts to preserve the confidentiality of Evaluation Material and Other Confidential Information even when disclosure is legally compelled. The changed text omits this 'provided that...' clause, removing the obligation to make such efforts. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which implies an overall confidentiality agreement and protection of sensitive information. The change permits disclosure without requiring efforts to minimize the breach of confidentiality, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In that case, or any time upon the request of a party for any reason, Vector will promptly, at Vector’s option (i) return to Captaris and/or (ii) destroy all Evaluation Material (and all tangible copies thereof) furnished to it or its Representatives by or on behalf of the other party pursuant hereto, and in either case no copies thereof shall be retained except that, any of the foregoing notwithstanding, that portion of the Evaluation Material that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents prepared by Vector’s Representatives, such Representatives’ agents, attorneys or employees, and oral or electronic Evaluation Material will be held by such Representatives and kept subject to the terms of this agreement or destroyed to the extent practicable and permitted by law.
perturbation.changed_text: In that case, or any time upon the request of a party for any reason, Vector will promptly, at Vector’s option (i) return to Captaris and/or (ii) destroy all Evaluation Material (and all tangible copies thereof) furnished to it or its Representatives by or on behalf of the other party pursuant hereto, and in either case no copies thereof shall be retained.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exception clause regarding analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents, the contract now requires *all* Evaluation Material to be returned or destroyed, without exception. This creates an internal contradiction because earlier in the contract, it's acknowledged that Representatives will create such documents. The removal makes it impossible for Vector's Representatives to retain material as required by their regulatory needs. It now requires that nothing should be retained, although that doesn't comply with legal or business practices.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In that case, or any time upon the request of a party for any reason, Vector will promptly, at Vector’s option (i) return to Captaris and/or (ii) destroy all Evaluation Material (and all tangible copies thereof) furnished to it or its Representatives by or on behalf of the other party pursuant hereto, and in either case no copies thereof shall be retained.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the exception clause present in the original text regarding analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents prepared by Vector's Representatives. This omission creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text expects that some Evaluation Material will be retained in these forms, while the changed text now mandates the return or destruction of *all* Evaluation Material without exception. The explanation explicitly states that this requirement does not comply with legal or business practices, highlighting the strong contradiction between the changed text and the expectations of the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (i) financial advisors to the parties may retain copies of the Evaluation Material and Other Confidential Information (including Evaluation Material and Other Confidential Information) stored on electronic, magnetic or similar media) in accordance with policies and procedures implemented in order to comply with legal and regulatory requirements and to demonstrate such financial advisor’s compliance with its obligations, and (ii) Representatives that are accounting firms may retain copies of the Evaluation Material in accordance with policies and procedures implemented by such persons in order to comply with applicable law, regulation, professional standards or reasonable business practice, and furthermore our Representatives that are accounting firms may disclose Evaluation Material to the extent required by law, rule, regulation or applicable professional standards of the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants, Public Company Accounting Oversight Board or state boards of accountancy or obligations thereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clauses (i) and (ii) which provide exceptions allowing financial advisors and accounting firms to retain Evaluation Material, the modified text contradicts the established practices of these professions and their legal obligations. It now states that no one can keep the material when they have to. This creates an in-text contradiction because each party and its Representatives, which includes financial advisors and accounting firms, will need to be bound by their legal requirements, which contradicts this provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, each party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides exceptions for financial advisors and accounting firms to retain Evaluation Material under specific conditions. The changed text removes these exceptions, stating that all parties and representatives are bound by confidentiality and other obligations, implying no one can retain the material. This creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text implies that parties are allowed to continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentiality which originally contained the exceptions, whereas the changed text eliminates such exceptions. The removal of clauses (i) and (ii) directly contradicts the original intent and practical application of the agreement, leading to an in-text contradiction, since it forces them to not be able to retain the information, which could be required by law.
